question.text: Which of the following is NOT part of the M&A regulatory framework in India?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The M&A regulatory framework in India includes Companies Act 2013, SEBI SAST Regulations 2011, FEMA 1999, RBI Act 1934, Income Tax Act 1961, Competition Act 2002, and SEBI Master Circular. The Consumer Protection Act 2019 deals with consumer rights and grievance redressal, not M&A transactions. **The Trap:** The Consumer Protection Act might seem plausible since mergers can affect consumers (product availability, pricing). But it is not part of the M&A regulatory framework — it addresses a different domain entirely. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX requires knowledge of the entire regulatory framework. The Competition Act 2002 is also tested in NISM Series XII for its role in market regulation. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** M&A transactions require multi-regulator clearance — Companies Act (NCLT), SEBI (SAST), CCI (competition), RBI (foreign exchange), and Income Tax (tax implications). Missing any one can stall a deal.
